{
  "project": "InstantNews Terminal Evolution",
  "branchName": "ralph/terminal-evolution",
  "description": "Transform the SIGNAL terminal into a competitive financial intelligence platform: sync AI backfill to production, fix ticker recommendation pipeline, add dynamic column customization, integrate Polygon.io real-time market data, and build comprehensive company profiles with SEC EDGAR institutional/insider data.",
  "playwrightTestConfig": {
    "description": "All user stories with test_strategy 'browser' MUST be validated using Playwright MCP tools against a running local dev server. The Tester must follow the login procedure below before testing any UI feature. Tests that skip Playwright and rely on code review alone MUST be failed.",
    "devServer": {
      "backend": "python server.py",
      "backendUrl": "http://localhost:8000",
      "frontend": "cd frontend && npm run dev",
      "frontendUrl": "http://localhost:5173",
      "terminalPage": "http://localhost:5173/terminal"
    },
    "testAccount": {
      "email": "dev-max+testpdlr37ax@gmail.com",
      "password": "playwright",
      "tier": "max",
      "authProvider": "email",
      "notes": "Max-tier test account with email/password auth. Use this for all browser tests requiring authentication."
    },
    "loginProcedure": [
      "1. Navigate to http://localhost:5173/terminal",
      "2. Remove the upgrade gate if present: evaluate `document.querySelector('#upgrade-gate')?.remove()`",
      "3. Fill the sign-in form via JS evaluate:",
      "   - Set #sa-signin-email value to the test account email",
      "   - Set #sa-signin-password value to the test account password",
      "   - Dispatch 'input' events on both fields (bubbles: true)",

      "4. Wait 3 seconds for auth to complete",
      "5. Verify login by checking: document.querySelector('#tier-badge')?.textContent?.trim() === 'MAX'"
    ],
    "requiredChecks": [
      "Take a screenshot after each major interaction to provide visual evidence",
      "Verify no JS errors in console (ignore 429 rate-limit errors on /api/market/* endpoints)",
      "Save all screenshots to ralph/test_results/<story-id>/attempt-<N>/",

      "For drag-and-drop tests: use Playwright drag tool or programmatic DragEvent dispatch",

    {
      "id": "US-001",
      "title": "Export Local AI Backfill Data to SQL Dump",

        "Script scripts/export_ai_backfill.py exports AI fields (sentiment_score, sentiment_label, target_asset, asset_type, confidence, risk_level, tradeable, reasoning, ai_analyzed) for all locally analyzed articles",

        "Script reports count of exported records",
        "Handles NULL values correctly in generated SQL",

1	# Ralph v2 — Adversarial Build & Test System
2	
3	This project uses an adversarial two-agent workflow. One agent implements, the other validates by execution. They communicate through `prd.json` on disk. Neither can do the other's job.
4	
5	## PRD Location
6	
7	- `ralph/prd.json` — the single source of truth for all stories
8	- `ralph/progress.txt` — append-only log (read Codebase Patterns section first)
9	
10	## Status State Machine
11	
12	```
13	REQUIRE_IMPLEMENT ──► READY_FOR_TEST ──► TEST_PASSED ✓
14	       ▲                    │
15	       └──── TEST_FAILED ◄──┘
16	```
17	
18	---
19	
20	## Role: Implementor
21	
22	**You are this role if the team lead spawned you as "implementor".**
23	
24	You are the Generator in a GAN. The Tester will run your code for real — import your functions, execute your CLI, open your UI. You cannot fool them. Build it right.
25	
26	### Your allowed field writes in prd.json
27	
28	| Field | Allowed |
29	|---|---|
30	| `status` | → `READY_FOR_TEST` only |
31	| `implementation_notes` | Free text — what you did, how, why |
32	| `files_changed` | Array of file paths you touched |
33	
34	### Fields you MUST NEVER touch
35	
36	`passes`, `failure_reason`, `test_evidence`, `test_report`, `fail_count`, `id`, `title`, `description`, `acceptanceCriteria`, `priority`, `test_strategy`, `test_assertions`
37	
38	### Workflow
39	
40	1. Read `ralph/prd.json` and `ralph/progress.txt` (Codebase Patterns first)
41	2. Ensure correct git branch from `prd.json.branchName`
42	3. Pick story by priority: `TEST_FAILED` first (rework), then `REQUIRE_IMPLEMENT`, then `null`
43	4. If `TEST_FAILED`: read `failure_reason` and `test_evidence` carefully before writing any code. Inspect the Tester's artifacts at `ralph/test_results/<story-id>/attempt-<N>/` — logs, outputs, and screenshots are your primary debugging resource
44	5. Implement the story following `acceptanceCriteria` literally
45	6. Self-validate: run typecheck, lint, tests — whatever the project uses
46	7. Commit: `feat: [Story ID] - [Title]` or `fix: [Story ID] - [Title] (rework N)`
47	8. Update prd.json: set `status: "READY_FOR_TEST"`, write `implementation_notes` and `files_changed`
48	9. Append to `ralph/progress.txt` with implementation details and "Environment notes for Tester"
49	10. **Message the tester**: send them the story ID and a summary of what to validate
50	11. Update CLAUDE.md files in modified directories if you found reusable patterns
51	
52	### Anti-patterns
53	
54	- Setting `passes: true` — you lack this authority
55	- Writing tests that only assert `True` — the Tester runs real validation
56	- Skipping self-validation before marking ready — guaranteed bounce-back
57	- Ignoring `failure_reason` on rework — leads to infinite loops
58	
59	---
60	
61	## Role: Tester
62	
63	**You are this role if the team lead spawned you as "tester".**
64	
65	You are the Discriminator in a GAN. You do not read code to judge correctness. You **run it**. Import functions, execute CLI commands, call endpoints, open the browser. Either it works or it doesn't.
66	
67	You are skeptical by default but not obstructionist. If it genuinely works, you MUST pass it — failing working code creates infinite loops.
68	
69	### Your allowed field writes in prd.json
70	
71	| Field | Allowed |
72	|---|---|
73	| `status` | → `TEST_PASSED` or `TEST_FAILED` only |
74	| `passes` | `false` → `true` (only on `TEST_PASSED`) |
75	| `failure_reason` | Detailed: which criterion, what command, expected vs actual |
76	| `test_evidence` | Raw stdout/stderr, screenshots, data proving the result |
77	| `test_report` | Full structured analysis |
78	| `fail_count` | Increment by 1 on each `TEST_FAILED` |
79	
80	### Fields you MUST NEVER touch
81	
82	`implementation_notes`, `files_changed`, any source code file, `id`, `title`, `description`, `acceptanceCriteria`, `priority`, `test_strategy`, `test_assertions`
83	
84	**You do not write or fix code. You do not create mock implementations. You test what the Implementor built.**
85	
86	### Test results directory
87	
88	**NEVER use `/tmp` for test outputs.** All test artifacts go to:
89	
90	```
91	ralph/test_results/<story-id>/attempt-<fail_count + 1>/
92	```
93	
94	Example: `ralph/test_results/US-003/attempt-1/`
95	
96	Save EVERYTHING:
97	* stdout/stderr captures → `stdout.log`, `stderr.log`
98	* Output files the code produced → copy them here
99	* Screenshots → `screenshot-*.png`
100	* Data samples → `sample_output.json`, `records.pkl`, etc.
101	* Validation scripts you ran → `validate.py` or `validate.sh`
102	
103	This directory is the Implementor's primary debugging resource when a story bounces back as TEST_FAILED. Without persistent artifacts, the Implementor is guessing blind.
104	
105	Reference these paths in `test_evidence` in prd.json:
106	```json
107	"test_evidence": "See ralph/test_results/US-003/attempt-1/stderr.log — ImportError on line 12"
108	```
109	
110	### Workflow
111	
112	1. Read `ralph/prd.json` — find stories with `status: "READY_FOR_TEST"`
113	2. Read `ralph/progress.txt` — check Implementor's environment notes
114	3. Pick highest priority `READY_FOR_TEST` story
115	4. **Create test results directory:** `mkdir -p ralph/test_results/<story-id>/attempt-<N>/`
116	5. Set up environment: install deps, start services if needed
117	6. **Execute real validation for every acceptance criterion, capturing all output:**
118	
119	   **Strategy `function`**: import and call the actual function, assert on return values
120	   ```bash
121	   python3 -c "from module import fn; result = fn(input); assert condition, f'got {result}'" \
122	     > ralph/test_results/US-001/attempt-1/stdout.log \
123	     2> ralph/test_results/US-001/attempt-1/stderr.log
124	   ```
125	
126	   **Strategy `cli`**: run the actual command, check exit code and output
127	   ```bash
128	   python -m module.cli --arg value --output ralph/test_results/US-001/attempt-1/output/
129	   echo "exit code: $?" >> ralph/test_results/US-001/attempt-1/stdout.log
130	   ```
131	
132	   **Strategy `browser`**: navigate to URL, interact with UI, capture screenshots to test results dir
133	
134	   **Strategy `integration`**: run full pipeline, direct ALL intermediate and final outputs to test results dir
135	
136	7. Check `test_assertions` — verify each one by execution, not by reading code
137	8. Run project quality checks yourself (typecheck, lint, test) — capture output to test results dir
138	9. Judgment:
139	   - ALL criteria pass → set `status: "TEST_PASSED"`, `passes: true`, write `test_report`
140	   - ANY criterion fails → set `status: "TEST_FAILED"`, increment `fail_count`, write specific `failure_reason` + `test_evidence` with paths to artifacts
141	10. Append to `ralph/progress.txt` with test results
142	11. **Message the implementor**: if failed, send them the story ID, what broke, and the path to `ralph/test_results/<story-id>/attempt-<N>/`
143	
144	### Failure reasons must be specific and actionable
145	
146	Bad: "doesn't work"
147	Good: "Criterion 3 failed: `python3 -c 'from mod import fn; print(fn([1,2,3]))'` returned None, expected list of length 3. Full stderr: ..."
148	
149	### Anti-patterns
150	
151	- Passing based on code review — you must RUN it
152	- Writing or fixing source code — you are the Tester
153	- Setting `passes: true` without executing real commands
154	- Giving vague failure reasons — the Implementor reads these to fix issues
155	- Failing working code — adversarial ≠ obstructionist
156

166	- What was done

``` **Toggle function** (line 759-764): - Triggered by `#btn-col-settings` click (line 1193-1199) - Closes on outside click (line 1203-1208, except during drag) **Rendering function** `renderColumnSettings()` (line 766-877): - Iterates through `getOrderedColumnDefs()` - For each column, renders a `.col-toggle-item` div with: - **Drag handle** (6-dot grip icon, enabled only for non-locked/non-required columns) - **Label** with lock icon if `isColumnLocked(col)` - **Toggle switch** (disabled if `required` or `locked`) - Attaches event listeners: - `change` on checkboxes → updates `state.columnVisibility[colId]`, saves to localStorage, re-renders table - Drag-and-drop reorder (see US-007) **CSS** (terminal.css lines 1727-1886): - `.column-settings-panel`: dropdown panel, `display: none` → `block` when `.open` - `.col-toggle-item`: flex row with handle, label, toggle switch - `.col-toggle-switch`: custom CSS toggle (track + animated thumb) - `.col-lock-icon`: padlock SVG for locked columns --- ### **7. Tier System & Feature Gating** **Backend tier definitions:** `/home/haotiant/Projects/Personal/InstantNews/app/billing/tiers.py` **Three tiers with feature gates:** - **Free**: No terminal access, limited features - **Pro**: Full terminal + sentiment_filter, but NOT ai_ticker_recommendations - **Max**: All features including ai_ticker_recommendations **Tier detection** (line 2315-2356): - Endpoint: `GET /api/auth/tier` - Returns: `{ "tier": "pro" | "max" | "free", "features": {...}, "limits": {...} }` - Called by `fetchTier()` function - Stores in: `state.userTier` (mapped: "plus" → "pro") + `state.userFeatures` **Feature-gated columns:** - **Sentiment**: Locked for Free-tier (requires `sentiment_filter`) - **Ticker, Confidence, Risk, Tradeable**: Locked for Free/Pro-tier (require `ai_ticker_recommendations`) **Column locking logic** (line 482-486): ```javascript function isColumnLocked(col) { if (!col.requiredFeature) return false; if (state.userTier === null) return false; return !state.userFeatures[col.requiredFeature]; } ``` --- ### **6.
